figures.caption: Figure 1: Dyadic memory systems are inadequate for group memory, which demands joint modeling of the collective task flow and per-user memory states. The multi-party attributes, such as Theory-ofMind, also make this fundamentally harder than a multi-user extension of dyadic memory.
Table 2: Accuracy (%) of memory systems on GroupMemBench across six query categories. Best per column in bold, second-best underlined.
Table 8: Per-domain accuracy (%) on the Finance subset of GroupMemBench. Best per column in bold, second-best underlined.

Table 9: Per-domain accuracy (%) on the Healthcare subset of GroupMemBench. Best per column in bold, second-best underlined. The Ambiguity and Implicit columns include 0 % / 50 % ties across many baselines, reflecting the small per-cell sample size of those question types in this domain.
Table 4: Edge relations in the synthesis graph G. “Static” edges are created during graph construction; “dynamic” edges are added incrementally as messages are generated.
Table 3: Persona vocabularies. Tone and style values are paired by sampling without replacement so users have distinct (tone, style) combinations.

Figure 4: Performance–efficiency trade-off across the four domains. Each marker is one of six baselines (five agent memory systems plus GRAPHRAG): x-axis is ingestion cost (USD, log scale), y-axis is per-domain QA accuracy (%), and bubble area encodes on-disk storage in GB. Per-(baseline, domain) numerical values are listed in Table 11.
Figure 2: Overview of the GroupMemBench data synthesis pipeline .

Table 11: Per-domain ingestion cost (USD) and on-disk storage (GB) for the six baselines used in the trade-off analysis, corresponding to the bubble plot in Figure 4. Costs aggregate all LLM and embedding calls during ingestion (query-time calls excluded). The Avg. column is the unweighted mean across the four domains.
Table 10: Per-domain accuracy (%) on the Manufacturing subset of GroupMemBench. Best per column in bold, second-best underlined.
Table 7: Per-domain accuracy (%) on the Technology subset of GroupMemBench. Best per column in bold, second-best underlined.
Table 6: Per-domain statistics of the synthesized group conversations.

Figure 5: (Left) Failure-mode decomposition: each baseline’s 185 non-abstention questions per domain split into correct, reasoning failure, and retrieval failure. (Right) Retrieval recall vs. answer accuracy. Markers on the diagonal are retrieval-bottlenecked; below the diagonal indicates reasoning loss (gold surfaced but answered wrong); above indicates the system answered correctly without the gold message, typically by aggregating other ingested context.
